prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='FT_catboost', seasonality_champion='ONLY_CWT_svr', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.7041) and high seasonality variance (0.693), with models capturing trends and seasonality very differently. FT_catboost leads trend tracking while ONLY_CWT_svr excels at seasonality capture. Early horizon specialists differ from late horizon specialists, indicating temporal pattern differences. These conditions strongly support DBA combination which handles model disagreement and seasonal variance optimally.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "FT_catboost",
  "seasonality_champion": "ONLY_CWT_svr",
  "overall_champion": "THETA",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "NaiveSeasonal", "late": "THETA"},
  "tier1_models": ["THETA", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "ETS", "DWT_rf", "FT_rf", "rf", "ARIMA", "DWT_catboost"],
  "tier2_models": ["CWT_rf", "FT_catboost", "NaiveSeasonal", "CWT_svr", "ONLY_DWT_svr", "NaiveMovingAverage", "svr", "ONLY_FT_rf"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.7041,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "up", "down"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.7041) and high seasonality variance (0.693), with models capturing trends and seasonality very differently. FT_catboost leads trend tracking while ONLY_CWT_svr excels at seasonality capture. Early horizon specialists differ from late horizon specialists, indicating temporal pattern differences. These conditions strongly support DBA combination which handles model disagreement and seasonal variance optimally."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25", "best_per_horizon_by_validation"],
  "params_overrides": {"topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}, "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25": {"top_k": 3, "shrinkage": 0.25}},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.7041) and high seasonality variance (0.693). n_unique_winners=6 indicates diverse horizon specialists. THETA leads with RMSE=167.28 while ONLY_FT_svr lags at 304.09, creating large dispersion. With only 3 windows, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3 (top_k=3) and inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25 provide robustness via shrinkage. best_per_horizon_by_validation captures horizon-specific patterns. DBA handles phase-shifted predictions optimally."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current candidate set has good diversity: 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25), 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 baseline (dba_combination). No leakage detected - all candidates use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection or are baselines. Score margin between top2 is minimal (0.004). Universe leaderboards show ade_dynamic_error_beta0.8_trim variants lead on MAPE/SMAPE/RMSE but current set already has diverse types. No pruning needed - 4 candidates with different types is appropriate.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}
raw.statistician: {"add_names": ["ade_dynamic_error_beta0.8_trim0.6", "best_single_by_validation"], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "Current set has 4 candidates with good diversity (selection + weighted). Universe shows ade_dynamic_error_beta0.8_* variants beat current on MAPE/SMAPE (~0.2426 vs ~0.244-0.259) and RMSE (~198.16 vs ~198.2-199.9). best_single_by_validation has best RMSE in universe (197.089 vs 198.2+). n_windows=3 is low so keep current shrinkage approach. relative_spread_mean=0.18 < 0.25 so disagreement not extreme. Adding ade_dynamic_error_beta0.8_trim0.6 and best_single_by_validation improves without removing diversity.", "changes": ["added ade_dynamic_error_beta0.8_trim0.6 for better MAPE/SMAPE from universe leaderboard", "added best_single_by_validation for best RMSE from universe leaderboard"], "when_good": "n_windows >= 6 would allow ridge_stacking; relative_spread_mean >= 0.25 would favor dba/trimmed_mean"}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.7041) and high seasonality variance (0.693), with models capturing trends and seasonality very differently. FT_catboost leads trend tracking while ONLY_CWT_svr excels at seasonality capture. Early horizon specialists differ from late horizon specialists, indicating temporal pattern differences. These conditions strongly support DBA combination which handles model disagreement and seasonal variance optimally.

proposer.rationale: Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.7041) and high seasonality variance (0.693). n_unique_winners=6 indicates diverse horizon specialists. THETA leads with RMSE=167.28 while ONLY_FT_svr lags at 304.09, creating large dispersion. With only 3 windows, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3 (top_k=3) and inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25 provide robustness via shrinkage. best_per_horizon_by_validation captures horizon-specific patterns. DBA handles phase-shifted predictions optimally.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has good diversity: 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25), 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 baseline (dba_combination). No leakage detected - all candidates use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection or are baselines. Score margin between top2 is minimal (0.004). Universe leaderboards show ade_dynamic_error_beta0.8_trim variants lead on MAPE/SMAPE/RMSE but current set already has diverse types. No pruning needed - 4 candidates with different types is appropriate.

statistician.rationale: Current set has 4 candidates with good diversity (selection + weighted). Universe shows ade_dynamic_error_beta0.8_* variants beat current on MAPE/SMAPE (~0.2426 vs ~0.244-0.259) and RMSE (~198.16 vs ~198.2-199.9). best_single_by_validation has best RMSE in universe (197.089 vs 198.2+). n_windows=3 is low so keep current shrinkage approach. relative_spread_mean=0.18 < 0.25 so disagreement not extreme. Adding ade_dynamic_error_beta0.8_trim0.6 and best_single_by_validation improves without removing diversity.
changes: added ade_dynamic_error_beta0.8_trim0.6 for better MAPE/SMAPE from universe leaderboard
added best_single_by_validation for best RMSE from universe leaderboard
statistician.when_good: n_windows >= 6 would allow ridge_stacking; relative_spread_mean >= 0.25 would favor dba/trimmed_mean
